memes.label: Ahlin
memes.description: Ahlin is a Swedish sculptor in Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — a member of the Scandinavian expatriate artist circle in Rome, known for his protective devotion to Francesca Cesca Jahrman and his emotional volatility

memes.label: Aventine
memes.description: The Aventine Hill in Rome is a site where Jenny Winge painted and later revisited with Helge Gram — a place of artistic creation and romantic memory in Undset's Jenny (1911)

memes.label: Berit Eck
memes.description: A Norwegian singer, formerly engaged to Hans Hermann, who marries him while already pregnant — her marriage becomes a painful event for Francesca Jahrman.

memes.description: German capital where Helge and Jenny cross paths and where Cesca briefly relocated from Rome

memes.description: Bodil Winge is a character in Undsets Jenny — Norwegian sibling

memes.label: Borghese Garden
memes.description: The Borghese Garden — the park surrounding the Villa Borghese in Rome — appears in the final chapter of Jenny (1911) as the setting for a scene of mourning two days after the funeral of Jenny's child

memes.label: Borghild
memes.description: Borghild is a character in Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — a friend of Cesca Jahrman based in Berlin, who appears through letters and reports as a link to the wider European social world beyond Rome

memes.label: Campagna
memes.description: The Campagna is the landscape surrounding Rome in Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — a space of romantic idyll, emotional release, and contrast with the city's social constraints

memes.label: Christmas at Via Vantaggio
memes.description: The Scandinavian expatriate community's Christmas celebration at Jenny Winge and Cesca Jahrman's studio on Via Vantaggio in Rome — Sigrid Undset's Jenny (1911), Part I Chapter VI, the novel's only holiday scene, depicting the displaced cultural rituals of Norwegian artists abroad

memes.label: Class and Poverty
memes.description: Economic reality of artists — financial precarity and class tensions in Jenny

memes.label: Colosseum
memes.description: The Colosseum — ancient Rome's great amphitheater — appears in the final part of Jenny (1911) as part of the ruined cityscape Jenny Winge moves through after her return to Rome

memes.label: Copenhagen
memes.description: Danish capital and Helge Gram's city of origin, where he studied and where Jenny confronts the Gram family

memes.label: Via del Corso
memes.description: The Via del Corso is the central thoroughfare of Rome — in Jenny (1911), it is the street where the expatriate artists walk, meet, and experience the city's social and emotional currents

memes.description: The artist circle as surrogate family — mutual support among expatriate creatives

memes.description: A dinner party held on Jenny Winge's twenty-eighth birthday at an osteria on the Via Appia Nuova in the Roman Campagna, marking a turning point in her relationship with Helge Gram.

memes.label: Norman Douglas
memes.description: Norman Douglas is a character in Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — a member of the Scandinavian expatriate circle in Rome who has a brief entanglement with Francesca Cesca Jahrman

memes.description: The experience of Norwegian artists living in Rome — the freedom, rootlessness, and cultural dislocation of expatriate existence in early 20th-century Europe

memes.description: The specific challenges and contradictions faced by women pursuing art in early 20th-century Europe

memes.label: First Meeting of Jenny and Helge
memes.description: The initiating event of Sigrid Undset's Jenny (1911) — Jenny Winge and Helge Gram's first encounter in Rome, which sets in motion the central relationship that will define the novel's trajectory across all three parts

memes.label: Frascati
memes.description: Frascati is a hillside town near Rome known for its wine — in Jenny (1911), it appears as a proposed and debated dinner destination during the expatriate artists' social evenings

memes.label: Frederikshald
memes.description: Frederikshald (Halden) is a Norwegian border town near Sweden — in Jenny (1911), it is the hometown of Cesca Jahrman's respectable family and a site of provincial moral judgment

memes.label: Gert Gram
memes.description: Gert Gram is a character in Undsets Jenny — Danish factory-owner

memes.label: Gunnar Heggen
memes.description: Gunnar Heggen is a Norwegian writer and Jennys closest intellectual companion

memes.label: Gunnar Proposes to Jenny
memes.description: Gunnar Heggen offers to marry Jenny Winge and protect her and her unborn child in Part II of Sigrid Undset's Jenny (1911) — the alternative future that Jenny cannot accept, representing the novel's central conflict between love, independence, and the social necessity of marriage

memes.label: Hans Hermann
memes.description: Hans Hermann is a character in Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — Cesca Jahrman's former lover who appears in backstory as a betraying figure whose legacy haunts Cesca's subsequent relationships

memes.label: Helge Abandons Jenny
memes.description: Helge Gram writes to break off his engagement to Jenny Winge while she is in Norway in Part II of Sigrid Undset's Jenny (1911) — the pivotal crisis that leaves Jenny pregnant and alone, transforming the novel's trajectory from romantic drama to tragedy

memes.label: Helge Gram
memes.description: Helge Gram is a Danish-Norwegian archaeologist and Jennys lover

memes.label: Hjerrild
memes.description: Hjerrild is a character in Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — a composer in the Scandinavian expatriate artist circle in Rome, known for his wit, socialist sympathies, and entanglement with Francesca Cesca Jahrman

memes.label: Holmenkollen
memes.description: Holmenkollen is a hillside area north of Christiania (Oslo) — in Jenny (1911), it is remembered by Jenny Winge from Rome as a place of beech groves and wild violets, a piece of Norwegian nature held in memory against the Roman landscape

memes.label: Holmestrand
memes.description: Holmestrand is a coastal town in Norway where Jenny Winge plans a restorative trip — and which becomes entangled in the emotional tensions between Jenny and Helge Gram in Part II of Undset's novel

memes.label: Ingeborg Winge
memes.description: Ingeborg Winge is a character in Undsets Jenny — Norwegian sibling

memes.label: Italy
memes.description: Mediterranean country, primary setting for Parts I and III of Sigrid Undset's Jenny

memes.label: Jenny Meets Helge's Parents
memes.description: Jenny Winge's introduction to Helge Gram's parents in Christiania — a strained meeting with Mrs. Gram followed by Gert Gram's unexpected visit to her studio, revealing the class and cultural tensions that foreshadow the relationship's collapse.

memes.label: Jenny Winge
memes.description: Jenny Winge is the protagonist of Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — a Norwegian painter struggling to reconcile her artistic ambitions with love, motherhood, and societal expectations

memes.label: Jenny and Helge's Engagement
memes.description: Jenny Winge and Helge Gram's engagement in Part I of Sigrid Undset's Jenny (1911) — the pivotal commitment that binds the novel's central couple at the height of their Roman spring, setting in motion the expectations, obligations, and eventual dissolution that structure the tragedy to come

memes.description: The culminating event of Sigrid Undset's Jenny (1911) — Jenny Winge's suicide in Part III following the death of her child and the collapse of her artistic and personal identity

memes.label: Lennart
memes.description: Lennart is a Swedish painter in Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — a member of the Scandinavian artist circle in Rome who appears in Parts I and III as a quiet, devoted presence

memes.label: Loulou Schulin
memes.description: A young Danish artist in the Rome expatriate circle, briefly married to a Norwegian author, who appears in Part III as a companion in Jenny's late-night escapades.

memes.label: Love and Independence
memes.description: The central thematic conflict in Sigrid Undset's Jenny — the tension between romantic fulfillment and personal autonomy, which drives Jenny Winge's tragedy and shapes every major character's choices

memes.description: Critique of conventional marriage and alternative relationship models in the novel

memes.description: The Medici Garden (gardens of the Villa Medici) in Rome offers a panoramic view of the city and its pines — in Jenny (1911), it appears as a site of aesthetic contemplation and a remembered landmark

memes.description: Scandinavian melancholy as pervasive atmosphere in the novel — the Nordic temperament in southern light

memes.description: Ponte Molle (Ponte Milvio) is a bridge over the Tiber River north of Rome's center — in Jenny (1911), a site of a pivotal walk and emotional conversation between Jenny and Helge

memes.description: The role of Catholic church imagery and religious symbolism in Jenny's Rome and its contrast with secular Norwegian life

memes.label: Ripetta
memes.description: The Ripetta is a street and area near the Tiber in Rome where Helge Gram took lodgings — in Jenny (1911), it represents his attempt to establish a Roman life alongside the artists' community

memes.label: Rome
memes.description: Rome is the primary setting of Sigrid Undset's novel Jenny (1911) — the eternal city where Jenny Winge and the Scandinavian expatriate artist community live, work, and love across Parts I and III

memes.label: San Pietro
memes.description: San Pietro is a landmark in Vatican-City, featured in Undsets Jenny

memes.description: The recurring motif of spring and seasonal rebirth in Jenny — hope, renewal, and the tension between natural cycles and human tragedy

memes.label: Suicide and Despair
memes.description: The novel's culminating theme — Jenny's psychological unraveling after her child's death and her final act of suicide as the ultimate expression of despair

memes.label: Termini
memes.description: Stazione Termini is Rome's central railway station — in Jenny (1911), it appears in the novel's opening chapter as the destination Helge Gram searches for after losing his way in the city on his first night in Rome

memes.label: Tiber
memes.description: The Tiber River flows through Rome and serves as a recurring scenic and symbolic element in Sigrid Undset's Jenny (1911) — its waters mirroring the emotional currents of the characters' lives

memes.label: Trajan Forum
memes.description: Trajan's Forum — the ancient Roman imperial forum — appears in Jenny (1911) as the site where Helge Gram first draws Jenny Winge close, a moment of romantic initiation set among ruins

memes.label: Via Appia Nuova
memes.description: The Via Appia Nuova is a Roman road leading out toward the Campagna — in Jenny (1911), it is the site of Jenny Winge's birthday dinner, a pivotal gathering of the Scandinavian expatriate circle

memes.description: Via Vantaggio is a street in Rome, featured in Undsets Jenny

memes.label: Wergelandsveien
memes.description: Wergelandsveien is a street in Christiania (Oslo) near the Royal Palace — in Jenny (1911), it is the neighborhood of Jenny Winge's family home and the setting for her melancholy return to Norway in Part II
